title: genista cinerea subsp. obtusiramea
intro: Genista cinerea subsp. obtusiramea, also known as broom or Spanish broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues. Traditionally, Genista cinerea subsp. obtusiramea has been prepared as an infusion, tea, or tincture for medicinal purposes, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
